heading: Insert and View Data
body:
In [mongosh](mongodb-shell), run the following command:

```none
use gettingStarted
```

This command creates a new [database](manual/core/databases-and-collections) called
`gettingStarted` and points your [mongosh](mongodb-shell)
environment to that database.

In [mongosh](mongodb-shell), run the following command to insert
documents into your new database:

**Input:**

```shell
db.people.insertMany([
 {
   name: { first: 'Alan', last: 'Turing' },
   birth: new Date('Jun 23, 1912'),
   death: new Date('Jun 07, 1954'),
   contribs: [ 'Turing machine', 'Turing test', 'Turingery' ],
   views : Long(1250000)
 },
 {
   name: { first: 'Grace', last: 'Hopper' },
   birth: new Date('Dec 9, 1906'),
   death: new Date('Jan 1, 1992'),
   contribs: [ 'Mark I', 'UNIVAC', 'COBOL' ],
   views : Long(3860000)
 }]
);
```

**Output:**

```json
{
  acknowledged: true,
  insertedIds: {
    '0': ObjectId('65c28946edcfbff3c7ce90c4'),
    '1': ObjectId('65c28946edcfbff3c7ce90c5')
  }
}
```

> **Note:** You might see a different value for
> [ObjectId](manual/reference/bson-types),
> because it is a system-generated value.

This command creates a new [collection](manual/core/databases-and-collections) in your
`gettingStarted` database called `people` and inserts one
document into that collection.

To view one of the  documents that you just inserted into your
cluster, run the following command to search the
`people` collection for documents that have a
`name.last` value of `Turing`:

**Input:**

```json
db.people.find({ "name.last": "Turing" })
```

**Output:**

```json
{
  _id: ObjectId("65c28946edcfbff3c7ce90c4"),
  name: {
      first: 'Alan',
      last: 'Turing'
  },
  birth: ISODate("1912-06-23T04:00:00Z"),
  death: ISODate("1954-06-07T04:00:00Z"),
  contribs: [
      'Turing machine',
      'Turing test',
      'Turingery'
  ],
  views: Long("1250000")
}
```

> **Note:** You might see a different value for
> [ObjectId](manual/reference/bson-types),
> because it is a system-generated value.

> **Tip:** To learn more about querying data in MongoDB, see
> [Query Documents](manual/tutorial/query-documents).
